'''Jason Smith''' (born 14 March 1972) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian international]] representative {{rlp|fe}} or [[Rugby league positions#Loose forward / Lock forward|lock forward]], he played club football in Australia for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] (with whom he won the [[1995 ARL season|1995 Premiership]]), [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Canberra Raiders]] and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], and in England for [[Hull F.C.]] At his peak he was regarded as one of the game's greatest players.<ref>{{cite news
| last = Hadfield
| first = Dave
| title = Hull land 'major coup' as Smith extends stay
| work = [[The Independent]]
| place =London
| publisher = Independent News and Media Limited
| date = 3 July 2002
| url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/hull-land-major-coup-as-smith-extends-stay-647104.html
| access-date = 27 December 2009}}{{dead link|date=August 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

== Background ==
Born in [[Brisbane]], Jason Smith is the younger brother of [[Darren Smith (rugby league)|Darren Smith]] and was a [[Souths Logan Magpies|Brisbane Souths]] five-eighth/lock.

==Professional playing career==
===1990s===
Smith signed with Sydney's [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] as an 18-year-old in 1990.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} The following year Smith captained the club's [[President's Cup]] side to a [[grand final]] win over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Mobile yet strong, he displayed his talent as a ball-player in Canterbury's back row or at five-eighth and impressed as a resolute defender. His début for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in the [[1994 State of Origin series]] ended when he was concussed after a sickening clash of heads with rival forward [[Ian Roberts (rugby league)|Ian Roberts]]. Smith recovered to play in Canterbury's losing grand final side and was rewarded for his season's efforts with selection in Australia's 28-man Kangaroo squad. His form on the [[1994 Kangaroo tour]] was excellent and although he was the outstanding player in wins over Warrington and Bradford Northern in the week leading up to Third [[Test match (rugby league)|Test]] selection, he could not break into Australia's Test team. 1995 proved to be a turning point in Smith's career. After having originally signed with the Bulldogs to join [[Super League (Australia)|Super League]], Smith and three teammates ([[Dean Pay]], [[Jarrod McCracken]] and [[Jim Dymock]]) turned their backs on their club and signed with the [[Australian Rugby League|ARL]]. This move consequently ensured Smith a place in Queensland's State of Origin team, with whom he was named man-of-the-match in the second game of the [[1995 State of Origin series|1995 series]]. He also went on to play in all three Tests against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. After gaining a release from his Super League contract via the courts, Smith played in the Bulldogs' grand final victory over the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] before coming off the interchange bench in Australia's 16–8 win in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] final.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

Joining [[Parramatta Eels]] along with his defecting teammates in 1996 following year in order to remain with the ARL competition, Smith's representative career continued for Queensland and Australia (against Papua New Guinea) but his 1997 season was ruined by injury. At Parramatta, Smith's commitment never wavered despite a succession of injuries. In three successive preliminary finals (1998-00), the Eels faltered at the last hurdle to making the grand final and too often the young club's fortunes rested on his shoulders. In 1998, with rugby league re-united, Smith played in the final two Tests of the Trans-Tasman series, alongside his brother Darren, and was chosen as lock in the first international the following year, Australia's 20–14 win over New Zealand. In Game III of the [[1999 State of Origin series]], Smith was cleared of a controversial high tackle on opposing forward [[Mark Carroll (rugby league)|Mark Carroll]] that left the [[New South Wales rugby league team|Blues]]' prop heavily concussed. (Later it was revealed that Carroll's own hand, which he raised in a reflex action to protect himself in Smith's ball and all tackle, had struck him and done most of the damage). Smith's [[1999 NRL season]] concluded with three Test appearances in Australia's victorious [[1999 Rugby League Tri Nations|1999 Tri-nations series]] victory over [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in Auckland.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===2000s===
2000 saw Smith make his final appearance for Australia, in the 52-0 thrashing of the Kiwis in the [[ANZAC Test]]. That year he was also awarded the [[Australian Sports Medal]] for his contribution to Australia's international standing in rugby league. Declining to take on the Parramatta captaincy in his final year with the club, his career appeared to be winding down after injuries and suspension saw him miss much of the second half of the season. Smith left the Eels at the end of the year to play for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], captaining the side from 2002 to 2004.

2005 saw Smith back in Australia playing with the [[Canberra Raiders]]. He also turned down a request to make a State of Origin comeback for Queensland.<ref>{{Cite news
| last = Roy Masters
| author-link = Roy Masters (sport)
| title = Slater axing has fans all at sea
| work = [[The Sydney Morning Herald]]
| place = Australia
| publisher = [[Fairfax Media]]
| date = 5 July 2005
| url = http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/slater-axing-has-fans-all-at-sea/2005/07/04/1120329386754.html
| access-date = 14 October 2010}}</ref> 2007 saw a move North for Smith to the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], becoming the oldest player to compete in the [[2007 NRL season]] at age 35 years and 186 days.<ref>{{Cite news
| last = Toohey
| first = Barry
| title = Still some bite in old Mad Dog
| work = [[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]
| place = Australia
| publisher = [[News Limited]]
| date = 2 February 2011
| url = http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/still-some-bite-in-old-mad-dog/story-e6frfgbo-1225998290314
| access-date = 2 February 2011}}</ref> On 6 June, midway through the season, he announced that would retire at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite news|title=Jason Smith announces NRL retirement|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/Sport/Jason-Smith-announces-NRL-retirement/2007/06/06/1181089128838.html|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=2007-06-06|access-date=2007-09-24}}</ref> His final game was on 15 September in the Cowboys' semi-final victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. A leg injury saw him sit out the preliminary final loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>{{cite news|first=Steve|last=Mascord|title=Cowboys salute Smith, Bowman|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,22467731-5006066,00.html|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|date=24 September 2007|access-date=24 September 2007}}</ref>

==Post-playing==
Brisbane newspaper ''[[The Courier-Mail]]'' confirmed on 31 August 2007 that Smith was appointed to the position of assistant coach of the [[Souths Logan Magpies]] in the 2008 [[Queensland Cup]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} This put him in direct opposition with brother Darren, who was head coach of rival side, [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]]. Smith played in the [[2011 Legends of Origin charity match]] organised by former player [[Mark Geyer]] to raise money for [[2010-2011 Queensland floods|Queensland flood victims]]. He also moved to [[Toowoomba]] where he bought the iconic Mill Street Tavern and played for Toowoomba Brothers in the Toowoomba Rugby League competition in 2011. In November 2016, the tavern was destroyed by a fire which authorities described as “suspicious”.

In January 2014, Smith was charged with trafficking cocaine in south-east Queensland. After pleading guilty to eight charges of possessing more than two grams of cocaine, he was sentenced to three years’ imprisonment but released immediately on parole.<ref>{{Cite news
  | last = Cumming
  | first = Stuart
  | title = Qld State of Origin great, restaurateur on cocaine charges
  | work = [[The Chronicle (Toowoomba)|The Toowoomba Chronicle]]
  | place = Australia
  | publisher = [[APN News & Media]]
  | date = 6 January 2015
  | url = http://www.thechronicle.com.au/news/league-star-restaurateur-charged-cocaine-trafficki/2502698/
  | access-date = 20 February 2015}}</ref><ref>[http://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/former-nrl-star-jason-smith-opens-up-on-his-drug-despair/news-story/9708c812b896d0d70d3973bcf5e7f106 Fallen Origin star’s drug despair] ''The Courier Mail'' (subscription required)</ref>
